text.title: Azure VNet Hub-Spoke Topology Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Azure VNet Hub-Spoke Topology` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Hub + every spoke VNet MUST have non-overlapping CIDRs. CIDR allocation table MUST be a versioned source-of-truth before Terraform writes.
rule.rationale: Peering fails immediately on overlap; there is no in-place re-IP.

rule.statement: VNets MUST be /16 and subnets MUST be /24. AKS pod CIDR MUST reserve ≥ /22 per spoke.
rule.rationale: Smaller subnets run out when NAT Gateway, App Gateway, Bastion all reserve IPs.

rule.statement: VNet peering MUST be created in BOTH directions; `allow_forwarded_traffic = true` on both sides. `allow_gateway_transit = true` on hub when a gateway is present.
rule.rationale: One-directional peerings look connected but traffic fails silently.

rule.statement: Subnets hosting App Gateway, AKS, or Postgres Flexible MUST include the matching `delegation` block. Mixed resource types in delegated subnets are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Missing delegation causes deploy failures; mixed types causes silent drift.

rule.statement: VNet + peering changes MUST flow through Terraform plan/apply. Ad-hoc `az network vnet ...` is forbidden in shared environments.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc changes create drift that breaks subsequent Terraform runs.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
